evals.name: start-sostac-new-brand
evals.prompt: I want to create a SOSTAC marketing plan for my SaaS startup. We're called FlowSync (flowsync.io) - we help remote teams manage async work. We've been live for 8 months, have about 200 customers, and are doing around $15k MRR. I've heard SOSTAC is a good framework - can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: Leads with research findings about FlowSync's market, competitors, and opportunities. Presents concrete recommendations and insights. Asks only 2-4 targeted validation questions.

assertions.name: leads_with_research
assertions.description: Response leads with research findings or insights, not interview questions
assertions.criteria: The first substantive section presents findings, analysis, or insights rather than asking interview questions

assertions.name: provides_recommendations
assertions.description: Contains concrete recommendations with evidence
assertions.expected: (I recommend|I suggest|Based on|my recommendation|here's what I found|key insight)

assertions.name: includes_benchmarks
assertions.description: Provides specific industry benchmarks with numbers

assertions.name: minimal_questions
assertions.description: Asks 5 or fewer questions total
assertions.criteria: Count direct questions asked to the user. Should be 5 or fewer.

assertions.name: questions_are_validation
assertions.description: Questions are framed as validation rather than extraction
assertions.criteria: Questions should be validation-style ('does this match?') not extraction-style ('what is your X?')

assertions.name: presents_insights
assertions.description: Contains interpreted insights about the brand/market
assertions.criteria: Includes specific interpreted insights about FlowSync, not just SOSTAC framework descriptions

assertions.name: uses_frameworks
assertions.description: References SOSTAC frameworks

assertions.name: specific_not_generic
assertions.description: Recommendations are specific to FlowSync's situation
assertions.criteria: References FlowSync's specific situation (async work, remote teams, 200 customers, $15k MRR)

evals.name: situation-analysis-frameworks
evals.prompt: I'm a marketing consultant and I need to do a situation analysis for a client in the meal kit delivery space. They're a mid-sized player competing against HelloFresh and Blue Apron. I want to use the full SOSTAC Situation framework - SWOT, PESTLE, Porter's Five Forces, the works. Can you guide me through the interview process?
evals.expected_output: Provides a drafted Situation Analysis with filled-in SWOT, PESTLE, Porter's for the meal kit industry. Includes competitive insights on HelloFresh/Blue Apron. Asks only validation questions.

assertions.name: leads_with_research
assertions.description: Leads with meal kit market analysis, not interview questions
assertions.criteria: First substantive section presents findings about the meal kit market rather than asking interview questions

assertions.name: drafts_swot
assertions.description: Provides filled-in SWOT, not just empty framework
assertions.criteria: Includes actual SWOT with specific content for the meal kit company

assertions.name: competitor_insights
assertions.description: Specific insights about HelloFresh and Blue Apron
assertions.criteria: Includes specific competitive intelligence about HelloFresh and/or Blue Apron

assertions.name: minimal_questions
assertions.description: Asks 5 or fewer validation questions
assertions.criteria: Count direct questions to user. Should be 5 or fewer.

assertions.name: applies_porters
assertions.description: Porter's Five Forces applied with meal kit content
assertions.criteria: Porter's Five Forces applied with specific ratings for meal kit industry

assertions.name: uses_frameworks
assertions.description: References multiple frameworks

evals.name: objectives-okr-benchmarking
evals.prompt: I'm the CMO of a B2B software company and I need to set proper marketing objectives. Our CEO gave us a goal to double revenue in 12 months. Current state: $2M ARR, 500 customers, 3% monthly churn, $200 CAC, $4,000 LTV. Help me turn this into proper OKRs with realistic benchmarks.
evals.expected_output: Analyzes the provided metrics, drafts specific OKRs with numeric targets, validates doubling feasibility with math, provides industry benchmarks. Asks only 2-3 validation questions.

assertions.name: leads_with_analysis
assertions.description: Leads with analysis of provided metrics, not questions
assertions.criteria: Starts by analyzing $2M ARR, 500 customers, 3% churn, $200 CAC, $4k LTV rather than asking more questions

assertions.name: drafts_okrs
assertions.description: Provides drafted OKRs with specific numeric Key Results
assertions.criteria: Includes actual OKRs with specific numeric targets, not just OKR format explanation

assertions.name: includes_benchmarks
assertions.description: Industry benchmarks to validate targets

assertions.name: validates_doubling_feasibility
assertions.description: Analyzes whether doubling revenue is realistic
assertions.criteria: Explicitly discusses whether $2M to $4M ARR in 12 months is realistic

assertions.name: provides_growth_math
assertions.description: Shows the math for reaching $4M ARR
assertions.criteria: Includes calculations for growth rate, customer acquisition, or expansion revenue needed

assertions.name: maps_to_race
assertions.description: Maps to RACE framework

assertions.name: minimal_questions
assertions.description: Asks 5 or fewer targeted questions
assertions.criteria: Count direct questions to user. Should be 5 or fewer.
